openmeter/sink/flushhandler/ingestnotifi.purpose: Post-flush handler that transforms successfully flushed SinkMessages into EventBatchedIngest domain events and publishes them to Watermill's balance topic, enabling the balance-worker to recalculate entitlement burn-down per subject. This is the bridge between the ClickHouse write path and downstream entitlement processing.

patterns.name: FlushEventHandler wrapping
patterns.description: The handler struct is never exposed directly as flushhandler.FlushEventHandler. NewHandler wraps the internal OnFlushSuccess callback using flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler with an options struct that declares the handler name, callback, logger, and metric.Meter.
patterns.example: flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler(flushhandler.FlushEventHandlerOptions{Name: "ingest_notification", Callback: handler.OnFlushSuccess, Logger: logger, MetricMeter: metricMeter})

patterns.name: HandlerConfig validation before construction
patterns.description: HandlerConfig.Validate() is called in NewHandler before any field assignment. NewHandler returns (flushhandler.FlushEventHandler, error) and propagates validation errors — callers must check the error.
patterns.example: if err := config.Validate(); err != nil { return nil, err }

patterns.name: Subject-level grouping and deduplication before publish
patterns.description: OnFlushSuccess groups EventBatchedIngest by namespace+subject key using lo.GroupBy, merges MeterSlugs with lo.Uniq, and chunks merged RawEvents to MaxEventsInBatch before publishing. Never publish one Kafka message per raw SinkMessage.
patterns.example: iEventsBySubject := lo.GroupBy(iEvents, func(event ingestevents.EventBatchedIngest) string { return event.Namespace.ID + "/" + event.SubjectKey })

patterns.name: MeterSlugs not MeterIDs in events
patterns.description: getMeterSlugsFromMeters extracts meter.Meter.Key (the slug) not the ID. Downstream open-source consumers have no access to IDs; always use Key.

patterns.name: Publish via eventbus.Publisher, not raw Watermill
patterns.description: All event publishing goes through h.publisher.Publish(ctx, event) where publisher is eventbus.Publisher. Never import watermill message types directly in this package.
patterns.example: if err := h.publisher.Publish(ctx, event); err != nil { finalErr = errors.Join(finalErr, err) }

patterns.name: StoredAt set once per flush batch
patterns.description: time.Now() is called once at the top of OnFlushSuccess and reused as StoredAt for all events in the batch, ensuring all events in the same flush have a consistent stored-at timestamp.

patterns.name: Nil Serialized guard before event construction
patterns.description: Events are filtered with lo.Filter to exclude SinkMessages where Serialized == nil before mapping. Any code path that builds EventBatchedIngest must guard against nil Serialized.
patterns.example: filtered := lo.Filter(events, func(event sinkmodels.SinkMessage, _ int) bool { return event.Serialized != nil })

key_file_guides.role: Implements the ingest notification flush handler. Owns the grouping, merging, chunking, and publishing logic. NewHandler is the only public constructor.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MaxEventsInBatch must be validated before use; missing validation lets zero/negative values cause infinite loops in lo.Chunk. StoredAt is approximated as time.Now() after flush — it is not the actual ClickHouse write timestamp.

key_file_guides.role: Defines EventBatchedIngest and its marshaler.Event compliance. EventVersionSubsystem is exported for use by balance-worker subscribers.
key_file_guides.watch_for: MeterSlugs must remain slugs not IDs. The var _ marshaler.Event compile-time assertion must be kept when adding new event structs. Event struct must stay a pure value type — no pointers to mutable shared state.

anti_patterns: Publishing one Kafka message per SinkMessage instead of grouping by namespace+subject and merging MeterSlugs
Using meter IDs instead of meter.Key (slugs) in EventBatchedIngest.MeterSlugs
Calling time.Now() inside the per-event loop — StoredAt must be set once per flush batch for consistency
Bypassing flushhandler.NewFlushEventHandler and returning the internal handler struct directly as flushhandler.FlushEventHandler
Importing raw Watermill message types directly — always publish through eventbus.Publisher

decisions.decision: Subject-level grouping with lo.GroupBy before publish
decisions.rationale: Balance-worker processes recalculations per subject; batching all meter slugs for a subject into one message reduces Kafka message count and ensures atomicity of balance recalculation triggers.

decisions.decision: MaxEventsInBatch chunking to cap RawEvents per message
decisions.rationale: Kafka has a max message size limit; unbounded RawEvents accumulation per subject would exceed it on high-volume subjects. Chunking keeps messages within safe size bounds.

decisions.decision: StoredAt approximated from time.Now() post-flush rather than per-event timestamps
decisions.rationale: ClickHouse write completion time is the semantically correct stored-at for downstream balance recalculation. Using a single now per batch is a fair approximation and avoids per-row timestamp tracking overhead.
